Karen tells me you export luxury jewelry and you are growing fast. I see a lot of pitches, so be direct with me. Why should I be your investor?
Happily. What sets us apart is the margin. It is our craftsmanship and our story that let us price above the market and still sell out. Last year revenue grew forty percent.
Forty percent is nice, but handmade rarely scales. How is this actually scalable?

Fair question. It is the bottleneck, not demand, that holds us back. We are raising one and a half million for a fifteen percent stake, at a valuation of ten million, and the money goes straight into capacity.
And the proof that this is not just a good year?

More than half of our buyers came back to order again. That repeat traction is the real proof. For a backer who understands premium brands, the return is clear. I would love to walk you through the numbers.
What proves the model is the traction, over half of our buyers reorder.
It is a fifteen percent stake we are offering, at a ten million valuation.
What sets us apart is the margin.

Hi Milton, it's Karen. I just got off a call with an investor I think you should meet. Her name is Diana, she runs a small fund that backs premium consumer brands, and she's genuinely curious about your business. I gave her the short version, and what really caught her attention was the margin and the repeat clients. She wants a fifteen-minute pitch, nothing fancy, just the story, the numbers, and what you'd do with the money. A word of advice from me: she will push back, hard, but it's not personal, it's just how she tests people. Be ready for one tough question about how scalable this really is. If you can show her the traction is real and the valuation makes sense, I think she'll lean in. I told her you'd send three slides by Friday. You've got this. Call me after and tell me how it went.

Thanks for the time, Diana. Let me keep this simple. We export luxury jewelry from Brazil to boutiques across the United States and Europe. What sets us apart is the margin, it's our craftsmanship and our story that let us price above the market and still sell out. Last year revenue grew by forty percent, and more than half of our buyers came back to order again. That repeat traction is the real proof. Here's the ask. I'm raising one and a half million for a fifteen percent stake, at a valuation of ten million. The money goes into one thing, scaling production so we can say yes to the larger boutiques we keep turning away. It is that bottleneck, not demand, that is holding us back. For a backer who understands premium brands, the return is clear, we have the clients, we have the margin, and now we need the capacity. I'd love to walk you through the numbers.
